title: wrong results for group by queries in the presence of primary keys
poc: create table facts (a_id bigint, b_id bigint);
create table a (id bigint not null primary key, c_id bigint);
create table b (id bigint not null primary key, name varchar(20));
create table c (id bigint not null primary key, name varchar(20));
select b.name, c.id, c.name from facts left join a on a_id = a.id left join b on b_id = b.id left join c on c_id = c.id group by b.name, c.name, c.id;
alter table c drop constraint c_id_pkey;
key_bug_features: group by queries with primary keys in joins
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE facts (a_id bigint, b_id bigint, value double precision);
CREATE TABLE a (id bigint NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, c_id bigint, description text);
CREATE TABLE b (id bigint NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name varchar(20), active boolean);
CREATE TABLE c (id bigint NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name varchar(20), created_at timestamp);
SELECT b.name, c.id, c.name, SUM(f.value) FROM facts f LEFT JOIN a ON f.a_id = a.id LEFT JOIN b ON f.b_id = b.id LEFT JOIN c ON a.c_id = c.id GROUP BY b.name, c.id, c.name;
